commentary_text: The model favors Australia at 44%, while the sportsbook consensus favors the draw at 43%. The largest single gap appears on the Paraguay win: bookmakers price it at 33%, the model at 26%, a 7-percentage-point spread. This suggests the market is pricing in information—recent lineup changes, injuries, or form shifts—that the model's Elo foundation does not capture. The model's Elo ratings reflect historical strength but cannot see current-week developments; the market aggregates real-money flows across dozens of books and may reflect team news unavailable to the statistical baseline. The dashboard flags this divergence as a signal worth monitoring, not a verdict on whether either source has misprice the match.
